subject: Physics
chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: According to the principle of conservation of linear momentum if the external force acting on the system is zero, the linear momentum of the system will remain conserved. It means if the centre of mass of a system is initially at rest, it will remain at rest in the absence of external force, that is the displacement of centre of mass will be zero. A plank of mass $M$ is placed on a smooth horizontal surface. Two light identical springs each of stiffness K are rigidly connected to struts at the end of the plank as shown in the figure. When the springs are in their unextended position the distance between their free ends is $3 \ell$. A block of mass m is placed on the plank and pressed against one of the springs so that it is compressed to $\ell$. To keep the block at rest it is connected to the strut by means of a light string. Initially the system is at rest. Now the string is burnt.
Question: The maximum displacement of plank is
questions.solution:
Since the center of mass will remain at rest.
$\mathrm{m}(5 \ell-x)=M \cdot x$
$\mathrm{x}=\frac{5 \mathrm{~m} \ell}{(\mathrm{M}+\mathrm{m})}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q:
Two particles approach each other from very large distance as shown. (consider gravitational force between the particle is negligible)
If the least separation between the particles is $b$, what is the speed of $M$ in center of mass frame when particles are closest

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Two particles approach each other from very large distance as shown. (consider gravitational force between the particle is negligible)
If $\frac{\mathrm{Q}^2}{L M V^2}=18 \pi \varepsilon_0$, find the least separation between the particles
questions.solution:
At closest approach velocity perpendicular to line joining particles
Conserve kinetic + electric potential energy from large separation to closest approach
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph:A 'U' shaped uniform rod of each of side having length ' $\ell$ ' and mass ' $m$ ' is hinged from the one end of one of its limbs as shown.
Question: The $Y_{c m}$ of the given system is
questions.solution: Let the point of suspension is origin(O), then C.M. lies on vertical line when it is in equilibrium position. $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{x}_{\mathrm{cm}}=\frac{\mathrm{m}(0)+\mathrm{m}(\mathrm{L} / 2)+\mathrm{m}(\mathrm{L})}{3 \mathrm{~m}}=\mathrm{L} / 2 \text { and } \\ & \mathrm{y}_{\mathrm{cm}}=\frac{\mathrm{m}(-\mathrm{L} / 2)+\mathrm{m}(-\mathrm{L})+\mathrm{m}(-\mathrm{L} / 2)}{3 \mathrm{~m}}=\frac{-2 \mathrm{~L}}{3} \\ & \therefore \tan \theta=\frac{\mathrm{L} / 2}{2 \mathrm{~L} / 3}=3 / 4 \Rightarrow \theta=\tan ^{-1}(3 / 4) \text { or } 37^0 \\ & \mathrm{~d}^2=\left(\frac{2 \mathrm{~L}}{3}\right)^2+\left(\frac{\mathrm{L}}{2}\right)^2 \\ & \mathrm{~d}=\frac{5 \mathrm{~L}}{6}\end{aligned}$ Moment of inertia $\mathrm{I}=\mathrm{Mk}^2=4 \mathrm{~m}\left(\frac{\ell^2}{12}+\frac{\ell^2}{4}+\frac{\ell^2}{2}\right)-\frac{\mathrm{m} \ell^2}{3}=3 \mathrm{~m} \ell^2$ (Assume full square and calculate moment of inertia about origin and subtract the moment of inertia of fourth rod.) Time period of a physical pendulum $=2 \pi \sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{I}}{\mathrm{mgd}}}=2 \pi \sqrt{\frac{6 \ell}{5 \mathrm{~g}}} \mathrm{sec}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph:A 'U' shaped uniform rod of each of side having length ' $\ell$ ' and mass ' $m$ ' is hinged from the one end of one of its limbs as shown.
Question: If the system is displace by small angle then time period of oscillation is
questions.solution: Let the point of suspension is origin(O), then C.M. lies on vertical line when it is in equilibrium position. $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{x}_{\mathrm{cm}}=\frac{\mathrm{m}(0)+\mathrm{m}(\mathrm{L} / 2)+\mathrm{m}(\mathrm{L})}{3 \mathrm{~m}}=\mathrm{L} / 2 \text { and } \\ & \mathrm{y}_{\mathrm{cm}}=\frac{\mathrm{m}(-\mathrm{L} / 2)+\mathrm{m}(-\mathrm{L})+\mathrm{m}(-\mathrm{L} / 2)}{3 \mathrm{~m}}=\frac{-2 \mathrm{~L}}{3} \\ & \therefore \tan \theta=\frac{\mathrm{L} / 2}{2 \mathrm{~L} / 3}=3 / 4 \Rightarrow \theta=\tan ^{-1}(3 / 4) \text { or } 37^0 \\ & \mathrm{~d}^2=\left(\frac{2 \mathrm{~L}}{3}\right)^2+\left(\frac{\mathrm{L}}{2}\right)^2 \\ & \mathrm{~d}=\frac{5 \mathrm{~L}}{6}\end{aligned}$ Moment of inertia $\mathrm{I}=\mathrm{Mk}^2=4 \mathrm{~m}\left(\frac{\ell^2}{12}+\frac{\ell^2}{4}+\frac{\ell^2}{2}\right)-\frac{\mathrm{m} \ell^2}{3}=3 \mathrm{~m} \ell^2$ (Assume full square and calculate moment of inertia about origin and subtract the moment of inertia of fourth rod.) Time period of a physical pendulum $=2 \pi \sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{I}}{\mathrm{mgd}}}=2 \pi \sqrt{\frac{6 \ell}{5 \mathrm{~g}}} \mathrm{sec}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
